category_title: Secondary-aged students with low self-worth and avoidance of mainstream education
scenario_title: Academic pressure and feelings of inadequacy
minimal_context: The student and tutor are sitting in a quiet room looking at GCSE subject options for college applications.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Let's look at your learning plan for this term. We need to pick your GCSE subjects so you can get into a good college for the career you want.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: I know I need GCSEs to get to go to a good college... to get a career. But there's always going to be negative experiences.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: We just need to focus on the requirements. Which subjects do you think you can pass to meet the standard?

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: The school wanting that good reputation so choosing the normal kids... but then the people that are below that standard are like the outcasts.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: You just have to deal with the expectations and pressure if you want to succeed and get back on track.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: [sighs] If you can get through mainstream then congratulations basically... You either deal with it or you don't. I had to back away, it was too much. It's not fit for everyone.

dialogue_combined:
TUTOR: Let's look at your learning plan for this term. We need to pick your GCSE subjects so you can get into a good college for the career you want.

STUDENT: I know I need GCSEs to get to go to a good college... to get a career. But there's always going to be negative experiences.

TUTOR: We just need to focus on the requirements. Which subjects do you think you can pass to meet the standard?

STUDENT: The school wanting that good reputation so choosing the normal kids... but then the people that are below that standard are like the outcasts.

TUTOR: You just have to deal with the expectations and pressure if you want to succeed and get back on track.

STUDENT: [sighs] If you can get through mainstream then congratulations basically... You either deal with it or you don't. I had to back away, it was too much. It's not fit for everyone.

task.content: Engaging with the active and personalised nature of the learning curriculum
task.learning_goal: Recognise what they actually want to do in the future and realise that they need GCSEs to get to go to a good college

context.environment: Much smaller environment with increased space and 'calmness'

challenging_behavior_trigger.condition: When facing the perceived pressure and high expectations of the school environment
challenging_behavior_trigger.observable_behavior: Generalised their displeasure, sighs, and had to back away
challenging_behavior_trigger.internal_state: Feelings of inadequacy and perceived failure in not fitting in with 'convention' in comparison to peers
related_quotes: If you can get through mainstream then [sighs] congratulations basically. . .You either deal with it or you don 't. . .
I had to back away, it was too much.
The school wanting that good reputation so choosing the normal kids. . .but then the people that are below that standard are like the outcasts. . .

behavior_patterns.condition: When reflecting on the possibility of reintegration and future educational prospects
behavior_patterns.observable_behavior: Reflected passivity in how they accept future circumstances
behavior_patterns.internal_state: Sense of desperation, potentially depressive mental health profile characterised by low self-worth
related_quotes: They won 't let me back, that 's why I can 't. . . I don 't even care what school I go back to. . . I just want to go back to mainstream school. . .I 'd do anything. . .
There 's always going to be negative experiences. . .
